note: Largest US arms buyer. Weapons used in Yemen war bombing campaign that killed thousands of civilians. Arms sales temporarily paused under Biden, resumed under Trump.
controversies: Khashoggi murder, Yemen school bus bombing with Raytheon MK-82, 200,000+ Yemen deaths

note: AUKUS agreement is the largest arms deal in history. Australia will buy 3-5 Virginia-class nuclear submarines.
Japan doubled its defense budget in 2022. Largest US military host nation with 55,000 US troops.
28,500 US troops stationed in South Korea. Annual "burden sharing" payments of $1.1B.
UAE used US weapons in Yemen and Libya interventions. F-35 sale approved in 2020, frozen in 2021 over China/Huawei concerns.
Arms sales to Taiwan infuriate China. The Taiwan Relations Act requires the US to provide "arms of a defensive character." $19B in arms backlog.
Home to Al Udeid Air Base, the largest US base in the Middle East. $28B in arms sales despite hosting Hamas leadership.
Israel receives $3.8B/yr in military aid (10-year $38B MOU). Additional $14.5B emergency aid in 2024 for Gaza operations. Total US military aid since 1948: $310B+.
controversies: Gaza 2023-24: 45,000+ Palestinian deaths, accusations of genocide at ICJ, US weapons confirmed in strikes on hospitals, schools, refugee camps.

note: Poland became a top US arms buyer after Russia invaded Ukraine. Spending 4.7% of GDP on defense in 2025 — highest in NATO.
Germany increased defense spending after Ukraine invasion. $100B special fund announced in 2022.
India shifting from Russian to US weapons. $22B in deals since 2007. "Major Defense Partner" status since 2016.
Closest US military ally. Joint programs include F-35 and AUKUS. Hosts US nuclear weapons at RAF Lakenheath.
Receives $1.3B/yr in military aid since Camp David Accords (1978). Total aid: $50B+. Used US weapons to suppress 2013 protests.
US liberated Kuwait in 1991. Hosts Camp Arifjan with 13,000 US troops.
Key US naval logistics hub. Changi Naval Base accommodates US aircraft carriers.
US rebuilt Iraq's military after destroying it in 2003. Ironic: billions to arm the same military we spent trillions fighting.
controversies: Iraqi forces using US weapons collapsed against ISIS in 2014, abandoning billions in equipment. US-supplied Abrams tanks ended up in ISIS propaganda videos.

note: Arms sales surged after Morocco normalized relations with Israel (Abraham Accords). Western Sahara conflict ongoing.
NATO ally. F-35 program was partly designed with Norwegian requirements in mind.
NATO ally buying F-35s partly to maintain parity with Turkey (which was kicked out of F-35 program for buying Russian S-400).
Joined NATO in 2023 after Russia invaded Ukraine. Immediately ordered 64 F-35s — Finland's largest defense purchase ever.
NATO's eastern flank. Hosting US Aegis Ashore missile defense site at Deveselu — a major irritant for Russia.
Former Warsaw Pact country now buying US stealth fighters. Shows how far NATO expansion has reached.
Closest US ally. F-35 purchase delayed for years by politics. Canada a NORAD partner with integrated air defense.
Home to US Fifth Fleet (Naval Support Activity Bahrain). Tiny island nation of 1.5M people hosting the US Navy's Middle East headquarters.
Key US ally bordering Israel, Iraq, Syria. Receives ~$1.7B/year in US aid. Hosts 3,000+ US troops at Muwaffaq Salti Air Base — a base Iran attacked in 2026.
US returned to Philippine bases under Marcos Jr. after Duterte era chill. Critical for containing China in South China Sea.
US treaty ally since 1954. Annual Cobra Gold exercises. Balancing US and Chinese military ties.
Received $12B+ in US military/counter-narcotics aid since Plan Colombia (2000). Now the largest US military partner in Latin America.
Largest Muslim-majority country. US rebuilding mil-to-mil ties cut after 1999 East Timor massacre. F-15 sale is a strategic counter to Chinese influence.
NATO ally. Home to Volkel Air Base — one of six European sites storing US nuclear weapons (B61 bombs, officially "neither confirmed nor denied").
Home to NATO headquarters. Kleine Brogel Air Base stores US nuclear weapons. Belgium's parliament debated removing them — US objected.
Neutral country buying American stealth fighters. Controversial referendum narrowly approved ($6.5B purchase). Shows F-35 market dominance.
Contributed to Afghanistan and Libya campaigns. Trump famously offered to buy Greenland (Danish territory) — twice.
NATO ally kicked out of F-35 program for buying Russian S-400 air defense system. Turkey then built its own 5th-gen fighter (KAAN). Relationship remains tense.
controversies: Bought Russian S-400 despite US threats. Used US-supplied weapons against US-allied Kurds in Syria. Blocked Sweden's NATO membership for over a year.

note: Largest economy in Latin America. Chose Sweden's Gripen over F-35/F-18. US arms sales limited but growing under closer defense cooperation.
